title: Recruit + confirm a credentialed electrical SME (licensed/registered electrician or CEng/PE) + target jurisdiction/standards
context: No electrical content in repair-cafe-kits may ship without sign-off from a credentialed electrical expert (a licensed/registered electrician or a chartered/professional electrical engineer — e.g. Part P/NICEIC in the UK, state-licensed electrician in the US, or CEng/PE electrical). This task identifies and confirms that expert, records their credential and jurisdiction, and establishes their willingness to sign off and periodically re-validate content. If this role is not filled, Milestone M2 (electrical triage notes) does not proceed; M0–M1 deliver full value without electrical content.
objective: Identify, approach, and confirm a credentialed electrical SME willing to sign off electrical triage content and periodically re-validate it; record their credential, jurisdiction, and agreement; document target jurisdiction(s) and applicable standards that will shape the electrical content framing.
acceptanceCriteria: A credentialed electrical SME is confirmed: either a licensed/registered electrician (e.g. UK Part P/NICEIC, US state-licensed) or a chartered/professional electrical engineer (CEng/PE, electrical specialization); credential type and issuing body are recorded

The SME's jurisdiction and the applicable electrical standards framework (e.g. UK BS 7671/IET, US NEC, EU Low Voltage Directive) are documented, as these shape content framing and sourcing in content-012

The SME confirms in writing (email or equivalent) willingness to: sign off the electrical triage content as distinct from the author; periodically re-validate (at least annually or on material guidance change)
The outcome is recorded in a committed document in the repo with credential, jurisdiction, standards framework, and agreement date
If no suitable SME is identified and confirmed, the document records the search outcome and explicitly notes that M2 electrical content will not proceed until the role is filled
output: A committed document recording the confirmed electrical SME's credential, jurisdiction, applicable standards, and agreement date (or recording the search outcome and M2 block if unfilled).
